question: Will Bitcoin reach a new all-time high price before October 6, 2026?
resolution_criteria: This question resolves as **Yes** if before October 6, 2026, Bitcoin's all-time high (ATH) price listed on [CoinMarketCap](https://coinmarketcap.com/currencies/bitcoin/) is higher than \$126,198.07 and the date of that ATH price is between December 17, 2025 and October 6, 2026.
background:
Bitcoin (BTC) is the world's first and most widely adopted cryptocurrency. Its price has historically shown significant volatility, reaching an all-time high of \$126,198.07 on October 6, 2025 before falling (according to [CoinMarketCap](https://coinmarketcap.com/currencies/bitcoin/)).

The price of Bitcoin is influenced by multiple factors, including:

* Institutional adoption (e.g., Bitcoin ETFs, corporate holdings)
* Macroeconomic conditions (e.g., inflation, interest rates, monetary policies) 
* Regulatory developments (e.g., government restrictions, taxation, legal status) 
* Technological advancements (e.g., Layer 2 solutions, network upgrades)
* Market sentiment and speculation

Several financial institutions and analysts have provided forecasts for Bitcoin’s price:

* [Bitwise](https://bitwiseinvestments.com/crypto-market-insights/the-year-ahead-10-crypto-predictions-for-2026), [Standard Charter and Bernstein](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2025-12-09/bitcoin-bulls-standard-chartered-bernstein-pare-back-most-ambitious-forecasts?embedded-checkout=true) predicts BTC to reach a new ATH in 2026.
* [Forbes](https://www.forbes.com/sites/digital-assets/2025/12/16/serious-2026-3-trillion-crypto-collapse-fed-warning-issued-10000-bitcoin-price-predicted-as-crash-fears-swirl/) has warned of a possible huge crash in BTC price.

Bitcoin has been [priced below \$100,000 since November 13, 2025](https://coinmarketcap.com/currencies/bitcoin/historical-data/).
